seoTitle: Stroud Hill vs Stroud Road — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Stroud Hill and Stroud Road. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Stroud Hill vs Stroud Road: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stroud Hill and Stroud Road.
heading: Stroud Hill vs Stroud Road — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stroud Hill, New South Wales and Stroud Road, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stroud Road has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Stroud Hill (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stroud Hill or Stroud Road safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stroud Hill has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Stroud Road scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stroud Hill scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Stroud Road scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stroud Hill or Stroud Road?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stroud Road has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
